---

<!-- GENERATED BY hg-global-skill-sync.sh FROM ~/.claude/commands/input.md; DO NOT EDIT -->

Manage input devices (MX Master 4, controllers, MIDI). [user-provided arguments] can be:

- `status` (default) — Show BT devices, logid/makima service state, battery levels
- `reset` — Run mx-reset.sh to restore MX Master 4 to known-good state (logid + Solaar settings + makima restart)
- `bt scan` — Scan for discoverable Bluetooth devices
- `bt connect <name>` — Connect a paired BT device by name
- `bt disconnect <name>` — Disconnect a BT device
- `makima list` — List all per-app Makima profiles
- `makima add <app>` — Create a new Makima profile for an app (detect window class via hyprctl)
- `midi list` — List connected MIDI controllers
- `solaar` — Show current Solaar settings (torque, haptics, scroll mode)
- `solaar set <setting> <value>` — Change a Solaar setting

Use the input MCP tools (input_status, bt_connect, bt_disconnect, bt_scan, input_list_makima_profiles, input_set_makima_profile, midi_list_devices, input_get_solaar_settings, input_set_solaar_setting) to fulfill the request.

For `reset`, run: `~/hairglasses-studio/dotfiles/scripts/mx-reset.sh`
For `makima add`, first run `hyprctl activewindow -j` to detect the window class, then create a profile with sensible defaults (BTN_SIDE/BTN_EXTRA navigation).
